source_textbook.name: Cambridge IGCSE Mathematics (0580)
source_textbook.section: Number, Algebra, Geometry, Trig, Probability, Statistics, Matrices

extracted_concepts.name: significant figures
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Digits that carry meaning in a measurement

extracted_concepts.name: bounds
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Upper and lower limits of accuracy

extracted_concepts.name: simultaneous equations
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Two or more equations with same variables

extracted_concepts.name: gradient
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Rate of change of a line (rise/run)

extracted_concepts.name: equation of line
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: y = mx + c where m is gradient, c is y-intercept

extracted_concepts.name: sine rule
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C

extracted_concepts.name: determinant
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A scalar value from a square matrix

extracted_concepts.name: histogram
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Bar chart with continuous data and class widths

extracted_concepts.name: factor theorem
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: If f(a) = 0, then (x-a) is a factor of f(x)

extracted_concepts.name: remainder theorem
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: f(a) is the remainder when f(x) is divided by (x-a)

extracted_relations.evidence: Gradient defines the steepness of a line
Sine rule relates sides and angles of any triangle
Cosine rule generalizes Pythagoras to any triangle
Determinant is computed from a matrix
Factor theorem is special case of remainder theorem
Cumulative frequency is used to create ogives
Vectors can be represented as matrices
